macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Closes at 6,506 Down 1.5% Friday, Breaks Below 200-DMA After Fourth Weekly Loss
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 1.5% to 6,506.48 on Friday, marking its fourth straight weekly decline and lowest close since mid-September 2025, now 7.1% below its January high. The index broke decisively below its 200-day moving average, signaling heightened volatility amid tightening credit conditions.[1][2]

items.title: Fed Holds Rates at 3.5-3.75% with Hawkish Stance, Markets Price Out 2026 Cuts
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained rates at 3.5-3.75% with upwardly revised inflation forecasts and lower growth projections, interpreted as stagflation signal. Markets now expect potential rate hikes, with next cut pushed to autumn 2027 and 6.2% chance of April hike.[1][2]
items.source: Lance Roberts Substack, Atrina Capital Substack

items.title: Major Indexes Decline 1.9-2.1% for Week, Russell 2000 Enters Correction
items.summary: U.S. equities saw broad-based declines with major indexes down 1.9-2.1%, Magnificent 7 off 2.5%, and Russell 2000 in official correction territory. Volatility eased slightly with VIX down 1.5%, indicating orderly de-risking.[1][2]

items.title: Iraq Declares Force Majeure on Oil Fields Amid Hormuz Disruption, Oil Above $100
items.summary: Iraq's force majeure on foreign-operated oil fields confirmed broadening Hormuz disruptions, accelerating Friday selling after brief oil retreat below $100. Oil prices up 30% month-over-month, raising inflation risks and curbing Fed cuts.[1][6]
items.source: Lance Roberts Substack, J.P. Morgan Asset Management

items.title: Stock Market Correlation with Oil Prices Unstable, Not Reliably Negative
items.summary: Historical data shows unstable correlation between S&P 500 and oil price changes, ranging from positive to negative, challenging assumptions that rising oil is unequivocally bad for stocks. Energy sector gained 3% amid Iran conflict impacts.[5]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $73,000-$74,000 as Market Shows Bullish Momentum
items.summary: Bitcoin has recently risen through the $73,000-$74,000 range, driving bullish momentum in the crypto market. Traders are watching altcoins for potential outperformance amid upcoming ecosystem events and token unlocks.

items.title: Aave V4 Proposal Submitted for Ethereum Launch
items.summary: A major update for the DeFi protocol Aave, with the proposal to launch Aave V4 on Ethereum officially submitted. This could impact AAVE token dynamics amid broader market shifts.

items.title: LayerZero Unlocks $52M in ZRO Tokens Adding Supply Pressure
items.summary: Approximately 5.6% of LayerZero's outstanding ZRO supply, worth $52 million, was unlocked on March 20, potentially increasing market supply pressure.

items.title: Ethereum Targets $4,000 Amid Institutional Inflows and Staked ETFs
items.summary: ETH trades at $2,150 with bullish technical signals and $315 million weekly inflows into staked ETFs from institutions like BlackRock. Analysts eye 80% gains to $4,000.

items.title: Global Crypto Market Cap Hits $2.42T with 0.23% Daily Gain
items.summary: The total cryptocurrency market capitalization stands at $2.42 trillion, up 0.23% in the last 24 hours, per CoinMarketCap data.

items.title: Bitcoin Transitions to Long-Term Trading Range Targeting $90,000
items.summary: Analysis indicates Bitcoin moving from a bear channel into a trading range after a second leg down, with recovery toward $90,000 probable this year.

items.title: Stagflation Risks Rise in 2026, Boosting Bitcoin's Appeal
items.summary: Persistent inflation, slowing growth, and policy constraints signal stagflation, potentially leading Bitcoin to outperform as a scarce store of value.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: White House Releases National AI Policy Framework
items.summary: The White House issued a seven-pillar AI policy framework on March 22, 2026, urging Congress to preempt state AI laws, enhance child safety online, address copyright issues, and provide federal datasets in AI-ready formats for training models.

items.title: ThinkLabs and SCE Deploy AI Digital Twins for Grid Solutions
items.summary: ThinkLabs collaborated with Southern California Edison using physics-informed AI digital twins on Microsoft Azure to reduce grid energization analysis from 35 days to 90 seconds, addressing AI-driven power demands and outage risks.

items.title: OpenClaw's ChatGPT Moment Raises AI Commoditization Concerns
items.summary: OpenClaw's breakthrough is sparking worries that AI models are becoming commodities, similar to ChatGPT's impact, as highlighted in recent AI news briefs.

items.title: OpenAI Partners with U.S. Department of Energy on AI Research
items.summary: OpenAI teamed up with the U.S. DOE to advance AI applications in energy, climate modeling, and scientific research for sustainable innovation.

items.title: Chinese Labs Accelerate AI-Driven Robotics Investments
items.summary: Chinese laboratories are heavily investing in robotics enhanced by AI for perception, planning, and adaptability, targeting services, logistics, and elder care with novel architectures.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Pearl Abyss's Crimson Desert Sells 2 Million Copies on Launch Day
items.summary: Korean developer Pearl Abyss sold over 2 million copies of its new game Crimson Desert on its Friday release, marking a major milestone for the studio.

items.title: NVIDIA Announces DLSS 5 at GTC, Faces Backlash from Game Developers
items.summary: NVIDIA unveiled DLSS 5 during its GTC event, but developers from Ubisoft and Capcom expressed dissatisfaction despite the company's claims, sparking debates among gamers.
items.source: YouTube - Paul's Tech News / Insider Gaming

items.title: NVIDIA Reveals New Vera CPU for High Single-Core Performance
items.summary: At GTC, NVIDIA announced the Vera CPU designed for extreme single-core performance, though it is not yet coming to PCs.

items.title: PlayStation Shifts PC Strategy Amid 2026 Spring Gaming Trends
items.summary: PlayStation is adjusting its PC strategy, while NVIDIA sparks debates and Xbox teases future plans in ongoing $200 billion gaming industry evolution.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: PlayStation Network Experiences Major Outage on March 21, 2026
items.summary: PSN suffered a widespread outage affecting multiple services including account management, gaming, and PlayStation Store, with reports peaking in the US and Japan. Sony acknowledged the issues on its status page, and services appear to be recovering as user reports decline.

items.title: India's Ministry of Education Hosts Bharat Innovates Deep-Tech Pre-Summit at IIT Bombay
items.summary: The two-day event on March 21-22 showcases 137 deep-tech startups across 13 domains like semiconductors, space, biotech, and energy, selected from over 3,000 applications. Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan addressed the summit as part of a global accelerator for Indian innovation.
